summary: I read the current dark-mode toggle implementation (`shared/_theme_toggle.html.erb`, rendered from `_sidebar.html.erb` and `layouts/auth.html.erb`) and cross-checked it against `guides/ui-style-guide.md`, which reserves each page's top-right for a per-page primary action. That ruled out both a per-view placement and a viewport-fixed floating button (risk of overlapping those primary actions). I committed to introducing a single shared `shared/_topbar.html.erb` partial — a slim, full-width bar rendered once in each layout (`application.html.erb` and `auth.html.erb`) above the scrollable content, with the toggle right-aligned inside it, while removing the toggle from the sidebar header. This is a pure layout/markup relocation — no changes needed to the Stimulus controller or theme helper. The approach, alternatives considered, exact file diffs, and test-update notes are written to `.pipeliner/phases/02-plan/main/technical-approach-planner/output/approach.md`.
